name: Fastly, Inc.

profile.description: Fastly is a global edge cloud platform that enables developers to build, secure, and deliver digital experiences with ultra-low latency. The company operates a programmable content delivery network (CDN) with 353 Tbps of edge capacity, complemented by AI-driven security, edge compute, and observability tools serving enterprise customers worldwide.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Fastly's WebAssembly-based serverless edge compute platform delivers microsecond latency with innovative features including semantic AI caching that reduces infrastructure costs, and a traffic classification engine that distinguishes human users from AI bots [1]. Security revenue grew 32% YoY to $35.4M in Q4 2025, making it the fastest-growing segment [2]. The company's edge compute, Key Value Store, and Fanout capabilities position it as critical AI-era infrastructure, with 353 Tbps of global edge capacity and 150ms average purge time [3].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: New CEO Kip Compton (appointed 2025, formerly Cisco/Comcast veteran) brings operational discipline but Glassdoor ratings sit at 3.2/5.0 across 344 reviews, with employees citing culture decline and lack of diversity in the C-suite, most of whom came from Cisco [4]. The Fast Forward program provides free services to 100 open-source projects and nonprofits, serving 5 trillion requests in 2023 [5]. Leadership turnover (Compton is the third CEO since 2022) creates instability.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Fastly achieved record Q4 2025 revenue of $172.6M (+23% YoY) with a record 64% non-GAAP gross margin, and 2026 guidance of $700-720M represents ~14% growth [2]. RPO grew 55% YoY to $353.8M and net retention rate improved to 110%. However, the company remains unprofitable with -19.5% profit margin, -12.8% ROE, and forward P/E of 81.8x implies aggressive growth expectations [1]. Debt-to-equity of 46.3% is manageable.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI is a significant tailwind for Fastly: surging AI-generated traffic increases demand for edge delivery infrastructure, while AI bot management and content protection become critical security services [1]. CEO Compton frames Fastly as essential AI-era infrastructure: 'We believe it's important for content owners to be compensated for their content so that their model is sustainable' [3]. William Blair upgraded the stock specifically citing AI traffic growth positioning. Fastly's semantic caching and edge compute directly serve AI workloads [2].

infinite_game.summary: Fastly shows infinite game potential through its open-source commitment and mission to 'make the internet a better place,' but frequent CEO changes (three since 2022) and cultural challenges suggest the organization has not yet found its long-term footing under stable leadership.

just_cause.analysis: Fastly's mission to 'fuel the future of the web' with fast, safe, and engaging experiences, combined with the Fast Forward program donating services to open-source and nonprofits, reflects genuine purpose beyond profit [5]. CEO Compton's advocacy for content creator compensation in the AI era demonstrates principled positioning.

trusting_teams.analysis: Glassdoor's 3.2/5.0 rating and specific complaints about Cisco-dominated C-suite lacking diversity of thought, culture being 'only talked about on a surface level,' and employee surveys being ignored indicate significant trust deficits [4]. Three CEO changes since 2022 creates organizational instability and erodes team confidence.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Fastly openly competes with and learns from Cloudflare, Akamai, and AWS CloudFront, using their strengths to sharpen its own edge compute and security offerings. The company's differentiated WebAssembly approach was developed specifically to outperform rivals' traditional CDN architectures [3].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Fastly's evolution from pure CDN to edge cloud platform with security, compute, and observability demonstrates strategic flexibility [1]. The addition of AI-driven security and bot management shows willingness to adapt product strategy to emerging threats and opportunities.

courage_to_lead.analysis: CEO Compton's public stance on AI content compensation and the decision to guide free cash flow positivity in 2026 after years of losses shows leadership courage [2]. Guiding 2026 revenue $35-50M above consensus demonstrated confidence in the business trajectory.

investment_thesis: Fastly is executing a compelling turnaround under CEO Kip Compton, with Q4 2025 revenue up 23% YoY to a record $172.6M, gross margins expanding to 64%, and 2026 guidance of $700-720M beating consensus by $35-50M [2]. The company expects to achieve free cash flow positivity ($40-50M) for the first time, with non-GAAP operating profit doubling to $50-60M. RPO growth of 55% to $353.8M provides strong revenue visibility, and the net retention rate improvement to 110% signals customer expansion. AI-driven traffic growth is a structural tailwind that positions Fastly's edge infrastructure as increasingly essential [1][3].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on valuation and execution risk. The stock has already surged 112% in 2026, and at forward P/E of 82x and ~6.5x forward revenue, Fastly is priced for near-perfect execution [1]. The company remains GAAP unprofitable with -19.5% profit margin, and three CEO changes since 2022 raise legitimate questions about leadership stability. Glassdoor's 3.2/5.0 rating suggests internal culture issues could impede execution. The 14% revenue growth guide for 2026 represents meaningful deceleration from 23% in Q4 2025, and competition from Cloudflare (growing faster with better margins) and Akamai (larger scale) creates persistent market share pressure [4].
key_catalysts: First full year of free cash flow positivity ($40-50M) in 2026 would validate the path to sustainable profitability and attract new institutional investors [2]
AI traffic tailwind acceleration: if AI agents and LLM-powered applications drive edge traffic growth above company forecasts, revenue could exceed the $720M high end of guidance [1]
Security revenue reaching 25%+ of total revenue would improve margin mix and reduce CDN commoditization risk, with security already growing at 32% YoY [2]
key_risks: Valuation risk: at 82x forward P/E after a 112% YTD rally, any earnings miss or guidance cut could trigger a severe correction [1]
Leadership instability: three CEOs since 2022 and a Glassdoor-documented culture decline could impair talent retention and product execution [4]
Cloudflare competition: Cloudflare's faster growth, superior margins, and broader platform pose persistent competitive pressure on Fastly's core CDN business [3]
sources: Simply Wall St — FSLY revenue guidance analysis and stock performance, March 2026
Fastly Q4 2025 Earnings Release — Record revenue of $172.6M, 2026 guidance of $700-720M, February 2026
CIO/Computer Weekly — 'Fastly CEO plots course through AI and security', 2025
Glassdoor — Fastly reviews (3.2/5.0, 344 reviews), accessed March 2026
Fastly 2023 ESG Report — Fast Forward program and environmental data
